skill.name: sparc-methodology
skill.description: Apply the SPARC methodology to plan, build, test, review, and document software with Claude Flow orchestration patterns.
skill.summary: Guide software projects through SPARC phases with TDD, review, and multi-agent coordination.
skill.author: Claude Flow

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk crypto and command patterns, but review found the crypto hits are false positives in prose, front matter, and mode names. The real concern is documented command execution through npx and local Claude Flow examples; this is legitimate for the methodology but should be published with a warning to verify packages before running commands.

medium_findings.title: Documented External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill includes CLI examples that run npx claude-flow and npx claude-flow@alpha commands. These are documented usage examples, not hidden execution, but following them can execute third-party package code from npm or a local installation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are directly present in bash examples and include npm package execution. Risk is moderated because the skill file does not execute them automatically.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Support URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to GitHub documentation, GitHub issues, and the npm package page. These are transparent support links and show no evidence of data exfiltration or covert network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible resource links in a support section. No code sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto Detections Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer labeled many lines as weak cryptographic algorithms, but review found descriptive text, metadata, mode names, and workflow examples rather than cryptographic implementation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged lines show prose, front matter, and JavaScript-shaped documentation blocks. There is no cryptographic code or credential handling in the inspected file.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Terms Are Workflow Descriptions
low_findings.description: Static reconnaissance flags point to normal development workflow language such as testing, hooks, monitoring, and analysis. No evidence found of host scanning, port scanning, or network discovery behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are hook examples and TDD process steps. They do not contain scanning tools, target enumeration, or automated reconnaissance logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: NPX Package Execution Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents npx-based activation commands. Users should pin versions and review the Claude Flow package before executing these commands in sensitive repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and repeat across the document. They are operational instructions, but not an automatic execution path inside the skill.

content.value_statement: Complex software work often loses structure across planning, coding, testing, and review. This skill provides a phased SPARC workflow for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code projects.
actual_capabilities: Explains the SPARC phases from specification through completion.
Lists specialized modes for research, architecture, coding, testing, review, and documentation.
Provides examples for Claude Flow MCP calls and CLI activation.
Describes TDD workflows with red, green, and refactor steps.
Outlines orchestration patterns for sequential, parallel, and pipeline work.
Includes common workflows for features, bugs, performance work, and full pipelines.
limitations: It is a methodology guide and does not execute workflows by itself.
CLI examples require a trusted Claude Flow installation before use.
Project-specific architecture decisions still require human review.
Performance claims depend on the user environment and task design.

use_cases.title: Structure a New Feature
use_cases.description: Use SPARC phases to define requirements, design the feature, implement tests first, and complete review steps.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Agent Work
use_cases.description: Plan research, coding, testing, review, and documentation tasks across specialized agent modes.

use_cases.title: Improve Delivery Discipline
use_cases.description: Apply repeatable TDD, coverage, review, and documentation practices to reduce missed validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a SPARC Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SPARC methodology to create a phased plan for this feature: [feature]. Include specification, architecture, TDD, review, and completion steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Architecture Phase
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as the SPARC architect mode for this project: [project]. Define components, interfaces, data flow, risks, and validation checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Run a TDD Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SPARC refinement for [feature]. Write the red, green, and refactor tasks, expected tests, and review criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Full Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SPARC multi-agent pipeline for [initiative]. Assign modes, dependencies, shared memory needs, quality gates, and completion artifacts.

output_examples.input: Plan a user authentication feature with SPARC.
output_examples.output: A phased plan with requirements, architecture choices, failing tests, implementation tasks, review checks, and documentation deliverables.

output_examples.input: Coordinate debugging for a memory leak.
output_examples.output: A workflow that assigns analysis, debugging, regression testing, and review tasks with clear handoff points.

output_examples.input: Prepare a full delivery pipeline for checkout work.
output_examples.output: A sequence of research, architecture, coding, TDD, review, optimization, and documentation activities.

best_practices: Start with explicit requirements, constraints, and success criteria before code generation.
Use test-first implementation and keep review tasks separate from coding tasks.
Verify any CLI command examples in a trusted environment before running them.
anti_patterns: Do not skip specification and architecture when the task has unclear requirements.
Do not treat generated plans as a substitute for security and design review.
Do not run package commands from documentation without checking package source and version.

faq.question: What is SPARC?
faq.answer: SPARC means Specification, Pseudocode, Architecture, Refinement, and Completion. It organizes software work into clear phases.

faq.question: Does this skill write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It describes workflows and mode usage. Code changes require an AI tool or Claude Flow command to perform them.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The planning, TDD, review, and documentation workflows are useful in Codex even when Claude Flow tools are unavailable.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention command execution?
faq.answer: The skill includes documented CLI examples using npx and local Claude Flow commands. Review those commands before running them.

faq.question: Is it only for large teams?
faq.answer: No. Individual developers can use the same phases to keep feature work structured and test focused.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the feature goal, constraints, target stack, test framework, and any project rules that the workflow must follow.
